blocks.text: Trust tier means the isolation level of the runtime execution environment.

rows: daemon runs directly on user/operator host OS
OS-level container isolation such as Docker, containerd, or Podman
only when policy bundle explicitly allows
only when policy bundle explicitly allows
only when policy bundle explicitly allows and CI isolation is verified

items: Trust tier is decided from external signals during runtime registration.
Signals combine self-reporting, such as wrapper manifest or runtime env, with verifiable facts, such as cgroup or namespace isolation detection.
`Unknown` is rejected by capability gate G-S1.
Trust tier is not an input to `ProviderCapability` / `CapabilityFingerprint`, but changing it triggers capability reevaluation.
